perturbation.original_text: Kiromic is committed to bear all costs necessary for R&D, including all clinical development costs, according to the terms and conditions set out in point n. 3;
perturbation.changed_text: Kiromic is committed to sharing in the costs necessary for R&D, including all clinical development costs;
perturbation.explanation: This change removes the explicit reference to 'the terms and conditions set out in point n. 3,' creating ambiguity about how the costs will be shared. It contradicts the later reference to point n.3 in Molipharma's obligations and introduces uncertainty on which terms they are sharing costs on the R&D programs
perturbation.contradicted_text: - Molipharma is committed to bear all costs necessary for R&D, including all clinical development costs, according to the terms and conditions set out in point n. 3;
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that Kiromic would bear all costs according to point n.3. The changed text states that Kiromic will share in the costs but removes the explicit reference to point n.3. The contradicted text states that Molipharma will bear all costs according to point n.3. The change creates a contradiction because it is no longer clear under what terms Kiromic will share costs, while Molipharma's obligation is still explicitly tied to point n.3. This discrepancy creates ambiguity and potential conflict in how R&D costs are allocated, leading to an in-text contradiction due to the omission, as defined by the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties undertake to promptly notify each other about the achievement of the Scope, as mentioned in point 2 ("the Results") , that may be subject to Industrial and Intellectual Property Rights, within 30 days from the achievement of such Results and to cooperate in the evaluation of the existence of the necessary requirements for the patenting/registration of such Results.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties will collaborate as needed on any achievements, as well as cooperate in the evaluation of the existence of the necessary requirements for the patenting/registration of such Results.
perturbation.explanation: The removal of the phrase “The Parties undertake to promptly notify each other about the achievement of the Scope, as mentioned in point 2 (“the Results”) , that may be subject to Industrial and Intellectual Property Rights, within 30 days from the achievement of such Results” creates ambiguity about when the other parties will know of an achievement. By simply stating “The Parties will collaborate as needed on any achievements”, creates uncertainty around the timing of notifications and cooperation. This is a contradiction because later in the text it states that Economic rights are derived based on the research programs referred to in point 2, and by taking that phrase out of the section, there is no way to prove that the economic rights are viable because the parties aren’t notifying each other properly of the achievements.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The commercial rights arising from the research programs referred to in point 2 are divided as follows: Oncology All economics rights are solely owned by Kiromic Biopharma. Kiromic will grant to Molipharma the follows royalties: - *% of the realized turnover by the marketing of Ovarian Cancer research results in Italy; -7- - *% of the realized turnover by the marketing of Ovarian Cancer research results in Europe. Sars-cov-2 - The economic rights for Europa will be an exclusive ownership of Molipharma - The economic rights in the U.S. will be an exclusive ownership of Kiromic. - For the rest of the world, the economic rights will be divided as follows: *% Kiromic; *% Molipharma.
perturbation.justification: The omission of the notification requirement creates a contradiction. The original text mandates a prompt notification within 30 days regarding achievements subject to Industrial and Intellectual Property Rights. The changed text replaces this with a vague 'collaborate as needed,' creating uncertainty about the timing and method of notification. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text,' which outlines the division of commercial rights based on research programs referred to in point 2. Without a clear notification process, it becomes difficult to ascertain when these rights arise, potentially rendering the division of rights unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: Upon termination of the contract, the agreement set forth in clause 5 ("Intellectual property rights and prohibition of transfer to third parties") and clause 6 ("Economic rights") will remain into force.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon termination of the contract, the agreement set forth in clause 5 ("Intellectual property rights and prohibition of transfer to third parties") will remain into force.
perturbation.explanation: By removing clause 6 (“Economic Rights”), this creates an in-text contradiction. By removing this obligation, the economic rights, which were listed in clause 7, no longer have an obligation to be in place. It is unclear if economic rights are supposed to be followed after contract termination or not.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The commercial rights arising from the research programs referred to in point 2 are divided as follows: Oncology All economics rights are solely owned by Kiromic Biopharma. Kiromic will grant to Molipharma the follows royalties: - *% of the realized turnover by the marketing of Ovarian Cancer research results in Italy; -7- - *% of the realized turnover by the marketing of Ovarian Cancer research results in Europe. Sars-cov-2 - The economic rights for Europa will be an exclusive ownership of Molipharma - The economic rights in the U.S. will be an exclusive ownership of Kiromic. - For the rest of the world, the economic rights will be divided as follows: *% Kiromic; *% Molipharma.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that both clause 5 (Intellectual Property) and clause 6 (Economic Rights) would remain in force after termination. The changed text removes the reference to clause 6, stating only clause 5 remains in force. This creates a contradiction with the 'Contradicted Text' which details the 'Economic Rights' for various research programs. Since clause 6 is no longer in effect after termination per the changed text, it is unclear whether the 'Economic Rights' detailed in the 'Contradicted Text' are still enforceable after termination, thus creating an in-text contradiction as per the definition of 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction'.
